title: OFAC Economic Fury package designates 40 Iranian shadow-fleet shipping firms and tankers
summary: The US Treasury OFAC added approximately 40 vessels and shipping firms to the SDN list on April 24, 2026 under Economic Fury, naming tankers ANSHUN II, COVENIO, EDOR, LPG SEVAN, LYNN, and MIRAAN, in a major expansion of pressure on Iran's oil-export shadow fleet.
contents:
# OFAC Economic Fury Targets Iran Shadow Fleet

## Overview
On April 24, 2026 the US Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) issued the Economic Fury sanctions package, designating approximately 40 shipping firms and individual tankers tied to Iran's oil export network. The action expanded the SDN list with named tankers including ANSHUN II (IMO 9253117), COVENIO (IMO 9263227), EDOR (IMO 9259317), LPG SEVAN (IMO 9177806), LYNN (IMO 9352559), and MIRAAN (IMO 9242481).

## Sanctions Mechanics
- Asset blocking on US persons handling designated entities
- Secondary sanctions exposure for non-US firms transacting with designated tankers
- Coverage extends to operators, beneficial owners, and front companies
- Designations align with the President's National Security Presidential Memorandum 2 framework

## Shipping Market Impact
Tanker market analysts tracking sanctions-relevant flows note the package compounds enforcement strain on Iran's shadow fleet. The April 28 CENTCOM interdiction of OFAC-sanctioned tanker Stream (IMO 9569633), redirected near Chabahar after a probable ship-to-ship transfer near the Strait of Malacca, demonstrates active maritime enforcement. Charter rates for replacement tonnage rose as sanctioned vessels lost insurance coverage and port access.

## Strategic Significance
OilCfd and other shipping-intelligence accounts emphasize that 39 Iranian or Iranian-linked vessels have now landed in interdiction status since the April 13 US naval blockade declaration. Tanker tracking points to forced rerouting via Chabahar and reduced Kharg Island throughput as sanctions enforcement bites Iran's shadow fleet.
